qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary file write via screenshot and PDF endpoint output_path parameter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes local file inclusion via file:// URLs accepted by the API, allowing arbitrary file reads. This finding describes arbitrary file writes via the output_path parameter in POST /screenshot and POST /pdf. The root cause differs: the advisory root cause is acceptance of file:// URL scheme, while this finding's root cause is the output_path parameter passed unsanitized to open(). Different vulnerability class (file write via path traversal vs file read via file:// URL), different sink, different root-cause code path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via markdown endpoint allows access to internal services
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes local file inclusion via file:// URLs accepted by /execute_js, /screenshot, /pdf, and /html endpoints. This finding describes SSRF via http:// URLs to internal IP addresses, with the root cause being lack of internal IP/hostname filtering. The advisory root cause is acceptance of the file:// URL scheme, while this finding's root cause is lack of IP-based access controls for http:// URLs. These are distinct vulnerable code paths: file:// scheme acceptance vs internal IP reachability. The finding shares affected endpoints and references the same validate_url_scheme function, but targets a different missing guard.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes local file inclusion via file:// URL scheme acceptance in the Crawl4AI Docker API (/execute_js, /screenshot, /pdf, /html endpoints), where file:// URLs are passed to the headless browser to read arbitrary server files. Finding 1 (arbitrary-file-write-via-screenshot) describes a different vulnerability: arbitrary file writes via the output_path parameter in POST /screenshot and POST /pdf, with a different root cause (output_path passed unsanitized to open()). Finding 2 (ssrf-via-markdown-endpoint) describes SSRF via http:// URLs to internal IPs, with a different root cause (no internal IP filtering). Neither finding identifies the file:// URL scheme acceptance as the vulnerable logic.
evidence: Advisory root cause: /execute_js, /screenshot, /pdf, /html endpoints accept file:// URLs, enabling arbitrary file reads from the server filesystem.

Finding 1 root cause: output_path parameter in POST /screenshot and POST /pdf is passed unsanitized to os.path.abspath() and open(), enabling arbitrary file writes. Different sink, different input parameter, different vulnerability class.
Finding 2 root cause: No internal IP/hostname validation for http:// URLs, enabling SSRF to internal services. Different URL scheme, different missing guard, different impact.
Neither finding's exploit.py uses a file:// URL. Finding 1 uses http://localhost:11235/playground with output_path=/tmp/... Finding 2 uses http://localhost:8888/ as the target URL.
